dek.expected_meta_description: A blog post by Wesley Melo. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.
dek.expected_onpage_lede: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua, ut enim ad minim veniam.
dek.actual_dek: A blog post by Wesley Melo. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.
dek.note:
Dek field was populated from , not the on-page

. The on-page lede instead became body paragraph #1, so no prose was lost — but the canonical 'dek' role moved.

checks.paragraphProseDiff: All seven body paragraphs match verbatim. Both figcaptions survive as italic paragraphs (format:2, textFormat:2).

media.match: Documented intentional fidelity loss; both captions preserved.

scriptCarryover.verdict: safe — ld+json correctly stripped, no inline script in Lexical body

frontendRender.behavior: graceful — Next.js notFound() because [slug]/page.tsx filters status:'published' and post is 'draft'

alternateRoute.note: Route does not exist; canonical route is /blog/[slug].

payloadApi.wordCountNote: beforeChange hook counts from DB-resolved body; cosmetic only per importer notes

regressions.evidence:
Mapped dek = legacy , not legacy

. The on-page lede was relocated to body paragraph #1 — readable but a structural demotion.
/blog/bergen-assembly returns 404 because the post is in draft status. This is by design (the route filters status:'published'). To preview, use the admin or publish the post. No crash, no 500.
Two CSS-painted figures (shape-hero, shape-mid) had no ; mapper collapsed each to its italic caption. Both captions preserved verbatim.

punchList:
If we want the on-page lede to drive the dek, re-map: prefer

over when both exist.
Publish the post (status: published) to make /blog/bergen-assembly render publicly; current 404 is correct but hides the import from non-admin viewers.
wordCount=0 in DB is cosmetic; if listing pages display read-time from wordCount, trigger a re-save or backfill.
summary: Content and integrity are clean: all 7 body paragraphs verbatim, both figure captions preserved as italic, no <script> carried forward, ld+json correctly stripped, no 500s. The frontend route returns 404 only because the post is a draft and the public route filters on published — graceful, not a crash. Only real regression is the dek/lede source-of-truth shift, which loses no prose but changes which line plays the 'dek' role.
